stories.title: Trump is weighing all options on Iran's Kharg Island
stories.description: President Trump said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.

stories.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
stories.description: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.

stories.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks
stories.description: A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
stories.description: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.

stories.title: Over a dozen U.S. soldiers injured in attack on Saudi base as Iran-backed Houthis enter war
stories.description: An Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded at least 15 U.S. service members. Israel also said it intercepted a missile launched from Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen.

stories.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
stories.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Iran-backed Houthis join war with attack against Israel
stories.description: There are fears the Iranian proxy may attack shipping in the Red Sea, adding to global economic woes.

stories.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
stories.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.

stories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
stories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

stories.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
stories.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

stories.title: Video shows US missile likely used in deadly strike on Iranian town, experts say
stories.description: The analysts identified a Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) in footage of the strike on the southern town of Lamerd.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: France hands captain of suspected Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker one-year jail sentence
stories.description: A French court on Monday sentenced in absentia the captain of a suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker to one year in jail for failing to comply with orders to stop his ship. The vessel, which the French navy boarded in September before releasing the vessel, was thought to be tran...

stories.title: Ukraine signs defence agreements with Gulf Nations eager to fend off Iranian drones
stories.description: The Ukrainian president has hailed "historic" defence agreements with several Middle Eastern countries after his visit to the region last week. Volodymyr Zelensky has been seeking to capitalise on his military's drone-fighting prowess as countries like the United Arab Emirates an...

stories.title: Zelensky hails 'historic' defence agreements with Gulf states
stories.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Monday said he signed "historic" agreements with countries in the Middle East last week, including sharing Ukrainian expertise on downing drones as Iranian strikes continue to target Gulf countries. Ukraine has previously proposed an exch...

stories.title: US not 'postured' for another Iraq 2003: Iran maintains vast, 'high-quality military capability'
stories.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Michael Knights, Adjunct Fellow at The Washington Institute and Head of Research at Horizon Engage. He examines the evolving military dynamics between the US and Iran, focusing on the operational possibilities available to US forces and the stru...

stories.title: Asian economies scramble to find solutions to war's impact on oil markets
stories.description: Iran continues to cut off the strait of Hormuz as it retaliates for the US and Israel's campaign against the Islamic Republic. The strategy has been effective as it piles pressure on the United States to the end the war, with global energy prices continuing to soar. Over 80% of o...

stories.title: Asian economies most vulnerable to impact of Middle east war on oil markets
stories.description: More than 80% of the crude oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) that passes through Strait of Hormuz heads to Asia. With the war in the Middle East giving no sign of slowing, many parts of Southeast Asia have suffered fuel supply difficulties. Now, policymakers in the Asia-Pacific...

stories.title: US military building up in the Middle East as Trump looks for offramp in Iran
stories.description: President Donald Trump said he wanted to take Iran’s oil and suggested US troops could seize the country’s Kharg Island export hub, even as he added in comments published on March 30 that a deal with Tehran to end the war “could be made fairly quickly.” The buildup has since been...

stories.title: Iran are now 'calling the shots' over the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: US President Donald Trump says Iran has agreed to let 20 ships through the Strait of Hormuz - a sign, according to him, that talks with Tehran are moving forward. But he has also signalled to the US possibly taking over Kharg island, and a regional US military build-up has trigge...

stories.title: US mulls over ground offensive in Iran despite claims of talks
stories.description: US and Israeli strikes on Iran continue over a month after the joint campaign began. Washington is mulling a ground offensive to seize Iran's nuclear stockpile, which would be one of the riskiest in US history. Thousands of American army personnel arriving in the region over the ...

stories.title: Iranian leaders 'do not take any statements from Donald Trump seriously '
stories.description: The United States and Israel kept up their attacks on March 30 on the Islamic Republic, even as there were signs of progress in nascent ceasefire talks. But according to FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah, based in Tehran, Iranian authorities do not take statements from Trump seriously. He a...

stories.title: Occupying Iran's Kharg island could be 'dangerous and risky' for US forces
stories.description: More American forces are reaching the Middle East. Before their arrival, the U.S. military had already built up the largest American force in the region in more than 20 years. While State Secretary Marco Rubio said US objectives could be met without ground operations, President D...

stories.title: Rubio tells Al Jazeera Strait of Hormuz will reopen ‘one way or another’
stories.description: This is a breaking news story.

stories.title: Trump wants to ‘take Iran’s oil’: Can he, and what would that mean?
stories.description: Trump said US forces could seize Iran's oil export hub at Kharg Island as the war enters its second month.

stories.title: War on Iran disrupts Asia’s used-car exports to the Middle East
stories.description: The US-Israel war on Iran has lead to disruptions in Asia’s used-car exporting industry.

stories.title: Spain closes airspace to US planes involved in war on Iran
stories.description: The announcement comes after Madrid refused to let Washington use jointly operated military bases for the war on Iran.

stories.title: Why have the US and Israel bombed more than 75 Iranian police facilities?
stories.description: An Al Jazeera investigation finds internal security facilities in populated areas have been heavily targeted.

stories.title: Homes damaged as Iranian missiles trigger sirens across northern Israel
stories.description: Missile debris from Iranian launches struck parts of central and northern Israel, damaging multiple areas.

stories.title: Iran’s Foreign Ministry denies claims of US-Iran negotiations
stories.description: Iran’s foreign ministry denied claims of negotiations with the US.

stories.title: Trump ‘pretty sure’ of Iran deal, but can Pakistan-led efforts end the war?
stories.description: Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt set up a team for backchannel talks with the US and Iran.

stories.title: Schools, water, industry: What civilian targets have US, Israel, Iran hit?
stories.description: US and Israel have increasingly been hitting civilian sites in Iran as efforts to end the weeks-long war are under way.

stories.title: Iraq risks Arab ties as Iran-aligned groups strike Gulf nations
stories.description: Joint Arab condemnation of cross-border attacks exposes Baghdad's failure to control armed proxies on its soil.

stories.title: Trump wants to invade Iran to seize oil, calls US objectors ‘stupid people’
stories.description: The Trump administration has deployed US Marines in preparation for the possible takeover of Iran's key oil export hub.

stories.title: Dar hints at US, Iran talks as quad pushes peace off-ramp
stories.description: • Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt back efforts to create conditions for Washington-Tehran structured negotiations• FMs explore ways for permanent end to hostilities, insist war is ‘not in favour of anyone’• Deputy PM to leave for Beijing to meet Chinese foreign minister tomorrow ...

stories.title: Netanyahu restores access after Latin Patriarch blocked from Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem
stories.description: Israel said the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem would regain access to Christianity’s holiest site after the decision to block him from entering the Holy Sepulchre on Palm Sunday triggered international condemnation. Israeli police prevented Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa from ent...

stories.title: US, Iran maintain communication via intermediaries — Rubio
stories.description: The US Secretary of State also stressed that Washington welcomes a scenario in which "Iran is led by people with a different vision for the future"

stories.title: Iranian diplomat says international law is 'main victim' of US-Israel aggression
stories.description: According to Esmaeil Baghaei, attacks have targeted universities, as well as cultural and historical monuments included in the UNESCO World Heritage List

stories.title: US-Israeli strikes damage 2,280 residential buildings in southwestern Iran — authorities
stories.description: The authorities are working on a more detailed assessment to obtain a more accurate estimate of the damage

stories.title: US-Israeli war on Iran on verge of spiraling out of control — Global Times
stories.description: The US government, the newspaper notes, initially projected that the war against Iran would last "four to five weeks", and later repeatedly claimed it would "end soon", but the facts proved otherwise

stories.title: US to achieve goals of Iran operation in weeks, not months, says top diplomat
stories.description: Marco Rubio pointed out that the goals of the US operation remained unchanged, including destroying Iran’s air and naval forces and missile production capacity, as well as diminishing its missile launching capability

stories.title: FACTBOX: Iran moves to charge toll for passage through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The fee will be charged in accordance with a new law that the Iranian parliament is set to pass shortly

stories.title: US to destroy Iran’s power plants, oil facilities if talks collapse — Trump
stories.description: According to the US president, this would allow Washington to avenge the deaths of its servicemen and other US citizens who were killed after the Islamic Revolution in Iran, which took place 47 years ago

stories.title: South Korean company imports naphtha from Russia — agency
stories.description: On March 30, a special committee of the ruling Toburo Democratic Party met to address the economic fallout from the US-Iran conflict

stories.title: Russia condemns lethal attack on President of Iraqi Kurds Nechirvan Barzani — diplomat
stories.description: "Moscow is deeply concerned about the continuing armed confrontation in the Persian Gulf region and the growing number of attacks targeting administrative and civilian infrastructure," Maria Zakharova stated

stories.title: Iran has no plans to attack NATO, says Romanian defense chief
stories.description: Radu Miruta also believes that the US has been so far unable to achieve all goals of its Iran operation

stories.title: UK fines Apple over $500k for violating anti-Russian sanctions — OFSI
stories.description: The penalty imposed was for "making funds available to a designated person without a license"

stories.title: Iran blames Israel for attack on power, desalination plant in Kuwait
stories.description: According to Ebrahim Zolfaqari, the recent attack on a desalination plant in Kuwait, accompanied by accusations against Iran, is a sign of Israel's meanness and baseness

stories.title: Iran’s vice president threatens anyone attempting to seize republic’s islands with 'hell'
stories.description: Mohammad Reza Aref stressed that "any aggressor who encroaches on the territory of Iran, especially in the Persian Gulf and on Iranian islands, will face a decisive, regrettable, and irreversible response"

stories.title: Ukraine left in lurch as partners divert Patriot missiles to Middle East, Zelensky says
stories.description: Zelensky mentioned plans to provide autonomous boats to the Gulf monarchies amid Iran’s continued blocking of the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Israeli army reports attacking about 40 military production facilities in Tehran
stories.description: According to the IDF, the targets included a site where long-range anti-aircraft missiles were assembled

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
